messages.author: ViezeVingertjes
messages.text: I have a light version of MC repeater running on a Heltec Cubecell, the way i optimized its battery life was to always put it into sleep (wake on tx) if there are no messages in the transmit queue. Seems to work great although i havent done a accurate comparison yet between the before/after usage.
messages.content_html: I have a light version of MC repeater running on a Heltec Cubecell, the way i optimized its battery life was to always put it into sleep (wake on tx) if there are no messages in the transmit queue. Seems to work great although i havent done a accurate comparison yet between the before/after usage.

messages.author: recrof
messages.text: @Scott can maybe also chime in with ideas

messages.author: entr0p1
messages.text: @Random Citizen I'd be keen to get your thoughts on what I've done so far for power management in these commits: https://github.com/entr0p1/MeshCore/commits/powermgt-xiao-nrf52840/?author=entr0p1 For "headless" nodes - especially repeater and room server, I don't see why we couldn't also just sleep the node all the time and have a wake when the radio RX's a packet. The clock was one concern I had (since it won't be "ticking") but you can use the millis() counter built into the board to increment the RTC on wake so that solves it. I'm not sure how it would go on a sensor node in terms of the frequency at which they're polled and whether sleeping is worthwhile, havent really dived into sensors much.
messages.content_html: @Random Citizen I'd be keen to get your thoughts on what I've done so far for power management in these commits: https://github.com/entr0p1/MeshCore/commits/powermgt-xiao-nrf52840/?author=entr0p1 For "headless" nodes - especially repeater and room server, I don't see why we couldn't also just sleep the node all the time and have a wake when the radio RX's a packet. The clock was one concern I had (since it won't be "ticking") but you can use the millis() counter built into the board to increment the RTC on wake so that solves it. I'm not sure how it would go on a sensor node in terms of the frequency at which they're polled and whether sleeping is worthwhile, havent really dived into sensors much.

messages.author: ViezeVingertjes
messages.text: I havent tested it on the official firmware, but my bet would be that critical stuff like time should work like normally, as it also keeps the rx active for the wake its still doing cycles.
messages.content_html: I havent tested it on the official firmware, but my bet would be that critical stuff like time should work like normally, as it also keeps the rx active for the wake its still doing cycles.

messages.author: ViezeVingertjes
messages.text: I had the exact same behavior because it went to sleep before processing the queue, simple check to make sure there are no packets to process and its fixed.
messages.content_html: I had the exact same behavior because it went to sleep before processing the queue, simple check to make sure there are no packets to process and its fixed.
messages.reply_html:
che aporeps 🌮
Yeah I saw it the other day, they were having trouble with the repeater not repeating until the next packet came in. Always repeating delayed by one packet. I haven't looked again since then
